page: OVHcloud r3-16 is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs from the AMD EPYC Milan processor family, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, backed by 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a 50 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration lists a baseline bandwidth of 0 Gbps alongside 1 complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The AMD EPYC Milan processor delivers strong single-core performance, ranking in the top 25% for both stress-ng single-core CPU tests and Geekbench single-core scores, as well as Geekbench text processing. However, multi-core and parallelized workloads show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance falls in the average range, while the Geekbench multi-core score, software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing benchmarks are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block read and write operations, but cached small-block reads are weak.

The r3-16 instance offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution, though the low core count limits its utility for highly threaded applications. This tradeoff is evident in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks, which rank as weak, and static web serving throughput, which is poor. Large-model LLM inference is also constrained, with the 7B model prompt processing scoring in the bottom 10%. Consequently, this instance is best suited for lightweight memory-bound applications, small-scale caching layers, text processing tasks, and basic single-threaded data processing workloads.
description: OVHcloud r3-16 is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on the AMD EPYC Milan x86_64 architecture running at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB per core, alongside a bundled 50 GB NVMe SSD and 1 public IPv4 address. The processor features 32 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and SHA-NI instructions. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, though multi-core execution, software compilation, and database operations are weak. Static web serving throughput is poor, and baseline network bandwidth is 0 Gbps. While single-threaded gzip compression performs at average levels, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak. This instance provides a cost-effective profile for memory-intensive tasks with low parallelization requirements, making it suitable for lightweight caching, text processing, and single-threaded workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud r3-16 is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 50 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and NVMe storage for single-threaded, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs at 2.3 GHz
16.0 GB RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
Bundled 50 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Best suited for lightweight caching and single-threaded memory-bound tasks
